title: [FIRRTL] CheckCombCycles is not checking Instance Input Port Bouncing
body:
Consider the following circuit:

```
circuit Foo:
  extmodule Bar:
    input a: UInt<1>
  module Foo:
    output a: UInt<1>

    inst bar of Bar
    bar.a <= a
    a <= bar.a
```

Currently this does _not_ trip combinational cycle detection due to the cycle bouncing off the input port of an instance.

This produces a crash during a HW canonicalizer, but it should be caught in FIRRTL CheckCombCycles.

The SFC will catch this.

The relevant MLIR is:

```mlir
module {
  firrtl.circuit "Foo"  {
    firrtl.extmodule private @Bar(in a: !firrtl.uint<1>)
    firrtl.module @Foo(out %a: !firrtl.uint<1>) {
      %bar_a = firrtl.instance bar interesting_name  @Bar(in a: !firrtl.uint<1>)
      firrtl.strictconnect %bar_a, %a : !firrtl.uint<1>
      firrtl.strictconnect %a, %bar_a : !firrtl.uint<1>
    }
  }
}
```
